channel.title: Engineering@Microsoft on Daily Tech Articles Feed
channel.description: Recent content in Engineering@Microsoft on Daily Tech Articles Feed

item.title: How we built the Microsoft Learn MCP Server
item.description: 📢 Exciting news from Microsoft! In June 2025, we launched the Microsoft Learn Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server to streamline how AI agents access up-to-date Microsoft Learn documentation. This server allows tools like GitHub Copilot to retrieve information efficiently. With features like microsoft_docs_search and microsoft_code_sample_search, agents can now easily find relevant content without the hassle of traditional APIs. Discover more about this innovative approach and its benefits for…

item.title: Diagnosing instability in production-scale agent reinforcement learning
item.description: 🚀 Hugging Face has integrated the Post-Training Toolkit into TRL, enhancing diagnostics for production-scale reinforcement learning (RL) systems. This integration allows for better monitoring and control in long-running agent systems. It helps identify late-phase instability that can develop gradually, often going unnoticed until recovery options are limited. 📈 The article discusses a specific issue with tool-using agents trained using on-policy methods, revealing that traditional metrics can…

item.title: The Interaction Changes Everything: Treating AI Agents as Collaborators, Not Automation
item.description: At Microsoft Ignite 2025, the focus was on transforming AI agents into collaborators rather than just automation tools. Amanda Silver and Karl Piteira discussed how this shift enhances engineering workflows, allowing developers to work alongside intelligent agents like GitHub Copilot. One case study highlighted how a complex migration project was completed in under 2 hours with 80-90% accuracy, compared to the usual 4-6 weeks. This success stemmed from treating AI as a partner, not just a…
